summary: Sophia brought in her right Signia Styletto X as it had stopped charging. Moisture damage and debris were found and cleaned from the charging port. The device was tested and is charging normally again. Advised on care including using a drying kit overnight and keeping the aids away from moisture. Device was returned in working order. Reminded of next appointment.

Came in following a referral from his GP. He mentioned he often misses family members calling from another room and finds it hard to keep up in noisy places like restaurants. We ran a pure-tone audiogram today and the results showed mild to moderate high-frequency hearing loss in both ears. Went through the audiogram with him and talked through what it means day to day. He seemed keen to try hearing aids so we have booked him in for a demo next week.

Fitted bilateral ReSound OMNIA 9 demo aids, set gain to 80% in first-time user mode. Patient noticed clearer speech and was advised about adjustment period. Patient to take aids home and return for follow-up.

Michael came in today to go ahead with the purchase after a positive trial at home. Fitted bilateral ReSound OMNIA 5 aids and programmed them to his audiogram. Ran real-ear measurements and output was within a good range. Spent some time going through everything with him including how to put them in and take them out, charging, adjusting the volume, and getting the app set up on his phone. He picked it all up quickly. Booked him in for a follow-up in four weeks to see how he is getting on and make any adjustments if needed.

David came in for a fitting for his right ear only. Fitted a ReSound OMNIA 9 RIC to the right ear and programmed it to his current audiogram. He has worn aids before so he settled in quickly and knew what to expect. We talked through the main differences between his old device and the OMNIA 9, particularly the Bluetooth streaming and the updated noise management. He was happy with how everything sounded during the appointment and had no concerns. Booked him in for a review in six weeks.
